sections.title: A: Original passage gap fill practice
sections.instructions: Complete the original passage using the best answers for the gaps.
sections.passage:
Original passage for Review: Block 6 – Units 11 & 12

Mia joined a class project because the group wanted fresh practice for passage gap fill practice. The tutor wrote (1) ...... (2) ...... (3) ...... (4) ...... (5) ...... on the board and asked everyone to explain their choices. Later, the group compared (6) ...... (7) ...... (8) ...... (9) ...... (10) ...... with the rule from the lesson. The text is new, but it practises the same passage gap fill.

sections.title: B: Original matching practice
sections.instructions: Match each original prompt with the correct ending.

items.sentence: During the class project on Review: Block 6 – Units 11 & 12, Mia wrote ...... to complete the model sentence.
items.answer: G – in a law restricting the use of loud music in residential areas.

items.sentence: In an original matching item about Review: Block 6 – Units 11 & 12, the best completion is ...... .
items.answer: D – forward with any information they feel could help the investigation.

items.sentence: Sara checked the matching practice notes and added ...... to the example.
items.answer: A – off accidentally because of a fault in the wiring.

items.sentence: The online lesson included a short sentence about Review: Block 6 – Units 11 & 12: the missing part is ...... .
items.answer: H – down everything the witness described, noting every small detail.

items.sentence: During the school club on Review: Block 6 – Units 11 & 12, Nina wrote ...... to complete the model sentence.
items.answer: C – in by this scheme and have handed over significant amounts of money.

items.sentence: In an original matching item about Review: Block 6 – Units 11 & 12, the best completion is ...... .
items.answer: E – up a security van and was given a seven-year prison sentence.

items.sentence: Iris checked the matching practice notes and added ...... to the example.
items.answer: B – away with minor offences because the courts are already overloaded.

items.sentence: The language lab included a short sentence about Review: Block 6 – Units 11 & 12: the missing part is ...... .
items.answer: F – off with a warning, but made it clear there would be no second chances.
